question: I want to study the demographic in NYC. Can you find me the zip code of the NYC high school that was in the top 3 by overall progress score in two school years starting from the school year with the lowest total airport passenger count between 2011 to 2015 and had an A grade in the 2010-11 progress. Report the percentage of White people contributing to the racial makeup of the neighborhood whose primary ZIP code is the same as that high school based on data in 2010 census?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Summing Total Passengers across all airports and months for 2011-2015, the lowest total occurs in 2011 at 105878221.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2015 has the lowest total air passengers from port authority of New York and New Jersey airports? (Sum Total Passengers by Year across all airports and months; select the minimum.)

2.fact: In 2011-12, the top 3 high schools by overall score were: It Takes a Village Academy (98.7), Academy for Careers in Television and Film (96.6), Manhattan Village Academy (96.6).
2.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 3 by overall score in the school year starting from <node_1 answer>?
answer: Academy for Careers in Television and Film

3.fact: In 2012-13, the top 3 high schools by overall score were: It Takes a Village Academy (105.9), Academy for Careers in Television and Film (100.9), Brooklyn International High School (100.5).
3.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 3 by overall score in 2012-13?
answer: Academy for Careers in Television and Film

4.fact: In 2010-11, 109 NYC high schools had an A grade in Progress Report, including It Takes a Village Academy, Manhattan Village Academy, and Brooklyn International High School. Academy for Careers in Television and Film had N/A (no data) for 2010-11.
4.subquestion: Which schools from <intersection of node_2, node_3> had an A grade in the 2010-11 Progress Report?

5.fact: It Takes a Village Academy (DBN 18K563) is located at 5800 Tilden Avenue, Brooklyn, NY with ZIP code 11236.
5.subquestion: What is the ZIP code of <node_4 answer>?

6.fact: The racial makeup of the neighborhood was 1.3% (1,816) White, 88.7% (120,231) African American, 0.3% (366) Native American, 1.1% (1,480) Asian, 0% (45) Pacific Islander, 0.4% (523) from other races, and 1.6% (2,140) from two or more races based on 2010 census.
6.subquestion: What percentage does White people contribute to the racial makeup of the neighborhood whose primary ZIP code is <node_5 answer> based on data in 2010 census?

reasoning_chain: Node 3: Top 3 NYC high schools by overall score in 2012-13
Node 2: Top 3 NYC high schools by overall score in school year starting from 2011
Intersection(2,3) -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film}
Node 4: From intersection, keep schools with A grade in 2010-11 -> It Takes a Village Academy
Node 5: ZIP code of It Takes a Village Academy -> 11203
Node 6: White population percentage for neighborhood with ZIP 11203 -> 1.3
